name: Input Capture via Accessibility Service
description: Adversaries may abuse Android Accessibility Service to capture user input and screen content. This technique is used by banking trojans to intercept OTPs, harvest credentials, and perform overlay attacks without user awareness.
mitigations: Disable Accessibility Service for untrusted apps
MDM policy to restrict Accessibility permissions
detection: Monitor for BIND_ACCESSIBILITY_SERVICE in APK manifest. Alert on AccessibilityServiceInfo with eventType covering TYPE_VIEW_TEXT_CHANGED.
banking_relevance: Critical — directly enables OTP harvesting from banking apps
rbi_reference: RBI Circular RBI/2021-22/74 — Mobile Banking Security

name: Capture SMS Messages
description: Adversaries may capture SMS messages to obtain OTPs and 2FA codes used by banking applications. READ_SMS and RECEIVE_SMS permissions allow silent interception of incoming messages before the user sees them.
mitigations: Restrict SMS permissions on enterprise devices
Use authenticator apps instead of SMS-based 2FA
Monitor for apps requesting READ_SMS without clear purpose
detection: Flag any APK requesting READ_SMS or RECEIVE_SMS that also targets banking applications.
banking_relevance: Critical — RBI mandates OTP for all banking transactions; SMS interception breaks this security control
rbi_reference: RBI Master Direction on Digital Payment Security Controls 2021

name: Masquerade as Legitimate Application
description: Adversaries use SYSTEM_ALERT_WINDOW permission to draw overlay windows on top of legitimate banking apps, creating fake login screens that harvest credentials. The user interacts with the fake UI believing they are in the real banking app.
mitigations: Implement visual anti-phishing indicators in banking apps
detection: Flag SYSTEM_ALERT_WINDOW permission combined with banking app package name references.
banking_relevance: Critical — primary credential harvesting technique for banking trojans targeting Indian UPI apps
rbi_reference: NPCI Security Circular 2022-23 — Mobile App Overlay Protection

name: Download New Code at Runtime
description: Adversaries use DexClassLoader or PathClassLoader to load additional DEX payloads from remote servers at runtime. This technique bypasses static analysis tools that only inspect the original APK, allowing malware to update its capabilities post-installation.
mitigations: Network monitoring for DEX file downloads
Restrict ClassLoader usage via app signing policies
detection: Detect DexClassLoader, PathClassLoader, InMemoryDexClassLoader in DEX bytecode. Network traffic to raw DEX file URLs.
banking_relevance: High — allows banking malware to receive new overlay templates or updated C2 commands without reinstallation
rbi_reference: CERT-In Advisory CIAD-2023-0042 — Android Dropper Malware

name: Input Injection via WebView
description: Adversaries abuse addJavascriptInterface to expose native Android methods to JavaScript running in a WebView. Attacker-controlled web content can call Java code with device-level privileges, enabling remote code execution.
detection: Static: detect addJavascriptInterface. Dynamic: monitor WebView URL navigation to non-whitelisted domains.
banking_relevance: High — webview injection enables fake banking portal sessions inside trojan app
rbi_reference: RBI IT Framework for Banks 2021 — Secure Mobile App Development

name: Scheduled Task / Shell Execution
description: Adversaries use Runtime.exec() or ProcessBuilder to execute shell commands on the Android device. This is used for privilege escalation, file system access, or executing additional payloads.
mitigations: Static analysis scanning for Runtime.exec usage
detection: Detect Runtime.exec, ProcessBuilder.start, ProcessBuilder in DEX methods.
banking_relevance: Medium — used for persistence and privilege escalation that enables banking malware to survive app uninstalls
rbi_reference: CERT-In Best Practices for Android Security

name: Standard Application Layer Protocol
description: Adversaries use standard HTTP/HTTPS protocols to blend C2 traffic with legitimate web traffic. Hardcoded URLs in APK strings indicate callback endpoints for receiving commands or exfiltrating stolen credentials.
mitigations: Block hardcoded C2 IPs at firewall
detection: Extract hardcoded URLs from DEX strings. Flag non-CDN, non-known-good domains especially those using dynamic DNS.
banking_relevance: High — C2 endpoints receive stolen OTPs and credentials from compromised banking sessions
rbi_reference: CERT-In Alert 2023 — Banking Malware C2 Infrastructure

name: Application Discovery
description: Adversaries enumerate installed applications to identify specific targets. Banking trojans scan for Indian banking app package names to determine which overlay to deploy or which credentials to harvest.
detection: Flag APKs containing hardcoded Indian banking package names (com.boi, com.sbi, com.icici, etc.).
banking_relevance: Critical — banking malware uses this to deploy targeted overlays for specific Indian banks including BOI, SBI, ICICI, HDFC
rbi_reference: RBI Cyber Security Framework 2021 — Threat Intelligence Sharing

name: Location Tracking
description: Adversaries collect device GPS location data. In banking fraud contexts, location is used to evade fraud detection systems that flag transactions from unusual geographies.
mitigations: Restrict ACCESS_FINE_LOCATION for apps without clear need
detection: Flag ACCESS_FINE_LOCATION in apps that also request SMS or Accessibility permissions.
banking_relevance: Medium — location data is exfiltrated to help fraudsters mimic legitimate transaction locations

name: Obfuscated Files or Information
description: Adversaries obfuscate code to prevent static analysis. Techniques include dynamic class loading, string encryption, and reflection-based API calls that hide the true intent of the malware.
detection: Detect reflection usage, Base64-encoded strings, dynamic class loading, and encrypted string constants.
banking_relevance: Medium — obfuscation delays detection and incident response, increasing dwell time of banking malware
